title: Nyctocalos cuspidatum
intro: Nyctocalos cuspidatum, also known as the "Night-blooming Nyctocalos," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family and classified as a type of woody vine. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Nyctocalos cuspidatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nyctocalos cuspidatum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, as well as being used in the form of infusions and dried extracts.
taxonomy: Nyctocalos cuspidatum belongs to the family Asclepiadaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the milkweed family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphology. The genus Nyctocalos is further classified as part of the tribe Periplocoideae within the Asclepiadaceae family. The plant's classification is also supported by its unique features, such as its stem and leaf morphology. The classification of Nyctocalos cuspidatum is an important aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
